title: README with project positioning and comparison vs Storybook/Zeroheight/Decap
description: Write the repository's main README establishing what SystemBook is, what it explicitly is not, and how it compares to the closest alternatives, so a visitor can quickly decide if it fits their need.
acceptanceCriteria: README opens with a concise one-paragraph description of what SystemBook is and the specific problem it solves
A 'What this is not' section explicitly lists out-of-scope items (not Git-based, not multi-tenant, not a component library builder itself, no draft/review approval flow in MVP)

A comparison section/table contrasts SystemBook against Storybook, Zeroheight, and Decap CMS on the dimensions that matter (self-hosted vs SaaS, CMS vs Git-based, live component preview vs static docs)
README includes quick-start pointers (link to TASK-60's setup doc, link to the published Docker image from TASK-58)
README states the license (finalized in TASK-63) and links to CONTRIBUTING.md

steps.details: Summarize SystemBook using the PRD's section 1/3 content: open source, self-hosted, CMS-real (not Git-based) documentation platform with CI-built live component previews.

steps.description: Write the 'what this is not' section
steps.details: List explicit non-goals sourced from the PRD's backlog/assumptions section: no Git-based editing, no multi-tenancy in MVP, no draft/review approval flow, no automatic `.stories.tsx` migration.
Build a table comparing SystemBook, Storybook, Zeroheight, and Decap CMS across: hosting model, content editing model, live component preview support, and cost — sourced from PRD section 3.
Add a 'Quick start' section linking to `docs/setup.md` (TASK-60) and the GHCR image (TASK-58), plus a license badge/line and a link to `CONTRIBUTING.md` (TASK-63).
Cross-check every capability claim in the README against what's actually implemented by this point in the task list — do not describe backlog V2 items as available.

technicalNotes: This README is the first thing a prospective open-source user/contributor reads — prioritize clarity on what problem this solves over exhaustive feature listing.
